data.title: Bitcoin Mining Companies Increase BTC Sales Amid Industry Challenges
data.description:
Publicly traded Bitcoin mining companies have sold more Bitcoin (BTC) in the first quarter of 2026 than in all four quarters of 2025, reflecting tightening business conditions within the mining industry. According to Cointelegraph, these companies, including MARA, CleanSpark, Riot, Cango, Core Scientific, and Bitdeer, collectively sold over 32,000 BTC in Q1 2026, as reported by TheEnergyMag. This figure surpasses the 20,000 BTC sold in Q2 2022 during the crypto bear market triggered by the Terra-Luna ecosystem collapse, setting a new record for BTC miner sales in a single quarter, according to TheMinerMag.

The increased sales occur as hashprice, a critical metric for miner profitability that represents computing cost, remains at record low levels under $35 per petahash/second per day (PH/s), based on data from Hashrate Index. This $35 PH/s level is the breakeven point for many Bitcoin miners, especially those operating older mining machines. The current hashprice of approximately $33 PH/s per day places about 20% of the mining industry in unprofitable territory. Hashprice for BTC miners has been declining since July 2025 and now sits at about $33 PH/s per day, according to Hashrate Index.

The mining industry faces increased competition due to a rising hashrate, reduced block rewards, and macroeconomic challenges. The Bitcoin Miner Reserve, which tracks all BTC held by miners, has been gradually decreasing since 2023, as noted by CryptoQuant. Bitcoin miners collectively held over 1.86 million BTC at the end of 2023 but currently hold about 1.8 million BTC. Miners periodically sell portions of their BTC to cover operating expenses, but lower crypto prices and rising energy costs have forced some to offload coins they would have held in their corporate treasuries. Asset manager CoinShares anticipates further capitulation among higher-cost operators in the first half of 2026 unless BTC's price recovers significantly.

In contrast to the miners' selling, Bitcoin treasury companies like Strategy continue to acquire BTC. Michael Saylor, co-founder of the largest Bitcoin treasury company, indicated earlier this week that Strategy is purchasing more BTC as the price retreats from a local high of over $73,000 reached this week. "Think bigger," Saylor remarked on Sunday, sharing Strategy's BTC purchase history chart, which has become synonymous with imminent BTC acquisitions.

data.title: Public Crypto Miners Exceed 2025 BTC Sales in Q1 2026
data.description: Public crypto miners have sold over 32,000 BTC in the first quarter of 2026, surpassing their total sales for the entire year of 2025. According to NS3.AI, data from Hashrate Index indicates that the hashprice stands at approximately $33 per PH/s per day, which is below the $35 breakeven point for many miners. Additionally, CryptoQuant data reveals that the amount of BTC held by miners has decreased from over 1.86 million at the end of 2023 to about 1.8 million at the time of publication.

data.title: AI TRENDS | OpenAI Launches GPT-Rosalind for Life Sciences Research
data.description: OpenAI has introduced a new model named GPT-Rosalind, designed to support research in the life sciences sector. According to Jin10, the model will initially be available as a research preview to select enterprise clients.

data.title: ECB Official Cautions Against Premature Rate Path Signals
data.description: On April 17, ECB Governing Council member Nagel stated that releasing clear signals about future interest rate paths before this month's rate decision would be a mistake. According to Jin10, he mentioned that policymakers currently lack sufficient information to determine whether the surge in energy costs will keep inflation high in the long term, which will dictate whether a policy response is necessary. "We need to maintain flexibility, and it is inappropriate to assert that rates will move in a certain direction now. I am not ready to make a commitment in advance," Nagel said. Insiders revealed that the ECB is inclined to keep rates unchanged at this meeting, but in the rapidly changing environment, a rate hike is not ruled out. Nagel noted that Germany's economy performed reasonably well at the beginning of the year, but momentum weakened due to the impact of the Iran war, with economic growth expected to be 0.3 percentage points lower than previously forecast this year. He also warned that investor sentiment is overly optimistic. "The current market is relatively optimistic because of the widespread assumption that lasting peace will be achieved in the Middle East and energy prices will fall," he said. "The central bank's duty is to ensure that if this scenario does not materialize, it will not have spillover effects on financial stability."

data.title: Lido DAO Approves $20 Million Buyback as LDO Surges 30% in 30 Days
data.description: Lido DAO has sanctioned a $20 million buyback initiative, resulting in a 30% increase in LDO's value over the past month, even as other major DeFi tokens experienced declines. According to NS3.AI, the approved plan allows the DAO to utilize up to 10,000 stETH, approximately valued at $23 million, for the repurchase of LDO tokens. CoinGecko data indicates that LDO was trading at $0.42, marking a 12% rise in the last 24 hours.

data.title: Trump Administration Blocks Closure of Indiana Coal Plant
data.description: The Trump administration has denied a request from CenterPoint Energy to shut down a 60-year-old coal plant in Indiana. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the utility company had deemed the plant costly and unreliable. Despite these concerns, the administration's decision mandates the continued operation of the facility.

data.title: AI TRENDS | U.S. Law Firms Warn Against AI Chatbot Use in Legal Discussions
data.description: More than a dozen major U.S. law firms have issued warnings to their clients regarding the use of AI chatbots in legal discussions. According to NS3.AI, these firms caution that engaging in legal conversations with AI tools may compromise attorney-client privilege. This advisory comes in the wake of a February ruling by Judge Jed Rakoff in New York, which allowed prosecutors to use a fraud defendant's Claude chatbot interactions and 31 AI-generated defense documents as evidence in court.

data.title: Peru's Central Bank Governor: Premature to Raise Rates Amid War
data.description: Peru's Central Bank Governor has stated that it is too early to consider raising interest rates during the ongoing war. According to Jin10, the governor emphasized the need for caution in monetary policy decisions given the current geopolitical tensions. The statement reflects the central bank's approach to maintaining economic stability while monitoring the impact of the conflict on the global economy. The governor's remarks highlight the challenges faced by central banks in balancing inflation control with economic growth during uncertain times.

data.title: Sky Launches USDS and sUSDS on Avalanche with Transfer Limits
data.description: Sky has introduced USDS and sUSDS on the Avalanche network via Skylink, implementing a daily transfer limit of 5 million in either direction. According to NS3.AI, the Avalanche bridge became operational on April 13, with plans to increase the transfer caps on April 27. Additionally, native conversions between USDS and sUSDS on Avalanche are anticipated later in the second quarter of 2026.

data.title: Drift Protocol Investors File Lawsuit Against Circle Over $280 Million Hack
data.description: Gibbs Mura has initiated a class action lawsuit on April 14, representing investors of Drift Protocol who suffered financial losses due to a $280 million hack on April 1. According to NS3.AI, the lawsuit claims that Circle allowed attackers to transfer $230 million in stolen funds via USDC and CCTP over an eight-hour period without freezing the assets. The incident reportedly reduced Drift Protocol's total value locked from $550 million to below $250 million and resulted in losses for at least 20 other DeFi protocols.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Considers Visit for Iran Agreement Signing
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has indicated a potential visit to Islamabad if the Iran agreement is signed there. According to Odaily, this statement highlights the significance of the ongoing negotiations and the potential diplomatic engagement involved.

data.title: BNB Surpasses 630 USDT with a 1.31% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 16, 2026, 18:29 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 630 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 630.01001 USDT, with a narrowed 1.31% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Trump: If the Iran deal is signed in Islamabad, I might go.
data.description: US President Trump: I might go if the Iran deal is signed in Islamabad. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. President Trump: Agreement Could Lower Oil Prices and Inflation
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump stated that if an agreement is reached, oil prices will decrease, leading to a drop in overall prices and inflation. According to Jin10, Trump emphasized the potential economic benefits of such an agreement, suggesting it could alleviate inflationary pressures. The President's comments highlight the ongoing discussions and negotiations aimed at stabilizing the market and reducing costs for consumers.

data.title: CFTC Chair Moves Forward on Digital Asset Regulations Amid Leadership Concerns
data.description:
The Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) Chair, Michael Selig, announced his intention to proceed with rulemaking related to digital assets and prediction markets, despite the absence of additional commissioners. According to Cointelegraph, Selig addressed the House Agriculture Committee on Thursday, responding to concerns from ranking member Angie Craig about the lack of leadership at the CFTC, which typically operates with a bipartisan panel of five commissioners. Craig urged Selig to refrain from finalizing regulations while he remains the sole commissioner.

Selig emphasized the importance of continuing rulemaking efforts to ensure investor and consumer protections, stating, "In the interim, we cannot, for the sake of the American people, slow down in our rulemaking." He expressed his commitment to fulfilling his duties as appointed by U.S. President Donald Trump, despite the scrutiny from lawmakers over his unilateral leadership on rules favoring crypto and prediction markets. As of Thursday, President Trump had not announced any nominations to fill the vacant commissioner positions.

During the hearing, Selig reiterated the CFTC's plans to advance rulemaking, particularly concerning event contracts on prediction markets. He asserted the agency's "exclusive jurisdiction" over these markets, as companies face legal challenges at the state level related to sports betting laws and insider trading legislation. The CFTC's legal battle over prediction markets continues, with gaming authorities in several states filing lawsuits against companies like Kalshi and Polymarket for allegedly violating state sports betting laws.

New Mexico Representative Gabe Vasquez questioned Selig, highlighting the regulatory discrepancies between event contracts and state-level gaming. Vasquez accused the CFTC of exploiting "loopholes" to circumvent state laws, potentially depriving jurisdictions of revenue. He challenged the agency's role, asking, "Are we regulating real economic risk, or are we allowing prediction markets to steal billions of dollars in an unregulated free-for-all, with no consumer protection as Congress and the CFTC turns a blind eye?" Companies like Kalshi maintain that they fall under the CFTC's sole jurisdiction, a stance that has led to legal victories in states like Arizona and New Jersey, where judges recently blocked state actions against Kalshi.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Asserts Iran's Long-Term Nuclear Weapon Ban
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced a significant declaration regarding Iran's nuclear capabilities. According to Odaily, Trump stated that Iran will be prohibited from possessing nuclear weapons for at least the next 20 years or even longer.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Advocates for Lower Interest Rates
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has expressed his opinion that interest rates should be reduced. According to ChainCatcher, this statement was reported by Jinshi. The call for lower interest rates aligns with Trump's previous economic strategies aimed at stimulating growth.

data.title: Strategy's STRC Sales Potentially Fund $1.2 Billion in Bitcoin Purchases
data.description: Strategy's recent sales of STRC shares this week are estimated to have generated over $1.2 billion, potentially facilitating the acquisition of up to 29,914 BTC. According to NS3.AI, this development comes amid scrutiny from Coffeezilla, who released an 18-minute video critiquing the 11.5% dividend-paying preferred stock. Coffeezilla cautioned that STRC should not be considered an insured savings product.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Optimistic About Upcoming Iran Meeting
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced that the next meeting with Iran might take place this weekend. According to BlockBeats, he expressed optimism about reaching an agreement with Iran.

data.title: U.S. President Trump: Oil Prices Are About Half of Previous Expectations
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump stated that current oil prices are approximately half of what was previously anticipated. According to Jin10, this development comes amid ongoing discussions about global energy markets and their impact on the economy. The statement highlights the volatility and unpredictability of oil prices, which have significant implications for both domestic and international economic policies. Trump's remarks may influence future strategies in energy production and consumption, as stakeholders assess the broader economic landscape.
